enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of the song Kalamithu Kalamithu?

The song conveys a soothing and nostalgic theme, focusing on the tranquility and beauty of sleep.

Who composed the music for the movie Chitthi?

The music for Chitthi was composed by the legendary MS Viswanathan.

Who sang Kalamithu Kalamithu?

The song was beautifully rendered by P. Susheela.

What is the release year of the movie Chitthi?

The movie Chitthi was released in the year 1970.

What emotions does the song Kalamithu Kalamithu evoke?

The song evokes feelings of calmness and nostalgia, perfect for relaxation.
enrichedMetadata.summary: The song Kalamithu Kalamithu from the movie Chitthi is a soothing melody that captures the essence of tranquility and nostalgia. Sung by the legendary P. Susheela, the song beautifully describes the peacefulness of sleep and the gentle passage of time. The lyrics, penned by Kannadasan, add depth to the serene composition by MS Viswanathan. This classic piece evokes a sense of calm and is perfect for moments of relaxation or a peaceful night drive.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Experience the timeless charm of Kalamithu Kalamithu from the classic era of Tamil cinema. This soothing melody from the movie Chitthi beautifully captures the essence of nostalgia and tranquility. Let the enchanting voice of P. Susheela and the masterful composition of MS Viswanathan transport you to a world of serene emotions.
stanzaMeanings: For a woman, sleep comes twice in life: once at birth and once at death.
If you let go now, there will never be sleep. My dear, close your eyes, close your eyes.
Time is passing, close your eyes, my girl. If you miss this time, there will be no sleep, my girl, no sleep.
At four years old, then school games. All day, they ask to sing clear Tamil songs.
When you turn two, there is no sleep. They ask you to fight with seven challenges. Endless troubles.
Time is passing, close your eyes, my girl. If you miss this time, there will be no sleep, my girl, no sleep.
The young girl's mind changes, searching for the boy's face. When longing comes, what is sleep?
When she goes to meet the one she loves, if the father stops it, where is sleep? Where is sleep?
When the groom comes and touches the bride's saree, in the sweet moonlight, where is sleep? Where is sleep?
Time is passing, close your eyes, my girl. If you miss this time, there will be no sleep, my girl, no sleep.
Even after a thousand months, when a child is born, even when called 'mother', sleep goes away, sleep goes away.
Hands shaking, eyes closing, time comes searching. All the unseen sleep comes naturally, comes naturally.
Time is passing, close your eyes, my girl. If you miss this time, there will be no sleep, my girl, no sleep.
